title: Pittsburgh's Loudest Instagram Feeds
subtitle: Ten small-business accounts the city is showing up for, ranked this quarter.
angle: The Pittsburgh small-business Instagram accounts whose audience converts at the highest intensity this quarter, illustrated by one recent post each. Rank-only, no percentages.
intro:
Some Pittsburgh accounts have a few thousand followers and act like they have fifty. The list below tracks which small-business feeds are producing the most heat per follower this quarter, the accounts whose audiences actually show up, leave a comment, share a story, watch the whole reel. National chains and out-of-town profiles are filtered out. So is anything that read as a joke being indexed earnestly.

The leader is Spot of Coffee, the alley shop between Shadyside and East Liberty, which is operating with a follower count most coffee shops would consider a starter audience and posting like the room is full. Number two is Delanie's Coffee in Shadyside, off the strength of a single tagged honky tonk photo that did the kind of numbers a giveaway usually does. Page's, Pittsburgh's seventy-five-year-old ice cream institution in Arlington, lands at six on the back of one ice cream truck reveal that the Southside half of the city seemingly RSVP'd to.

What the list flags this quarter is who knows their audience and who is still posting into a void. Issue 02 will widen the pool, track how this list shifts, and start naming the accounts that hold their position quarter over quarter.
